notes: Marveling at a child's growth — common family-context use. Note: subhanallah on a child can be confused with masyaallah, which is the more conventional 'admiration without evil eye' expression for human attributes; subhanallah is more commonly for natural phenomena. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: whether this would actually be 'masyaallah' in conversational practice (case may be wrong fit for subhanallah — flag for fixture rewrite).
